q: According to Newton's second law, an object that is accelerating must have a ___ force acting on it.
An object moving in a circle, such as a ball on the end of a string, must therefore have a force applied to it to keep it moving in the circle. This force is known as the ___ force.
For uniform circular motion (speed $v = \text{constant}$), the acceleration is centripetal acceleration which is directed towards the ___ of the circle at any moment.
In the absence of this force, the object would not move along a circle but move in a ___ line, according to Newton's first law.
Using Newton's second law, $F_{\text{net}} = ma$, the magnitude of the centripetal force is $F_c = ma_c = ___$.
Substituting $v = r\omega$ in Eq. (2.1), $F_c = ___$.
For an object to move in a circle, a force is needed to deviate from its straight line path and accelerate towards the ___ of the circle.
The tension in the string provides the required centripetal force for the ball to keep moving in ___ path.
Some examples of the centripetal force are the gravitational force of the earth on the ___ orbiting the earth.
The gravitational force of the sun on the ___ orbiting the sun provides centripetal force.
The electric force acting on an ___ orbiting the nucleus acts as the required centripetal force.
When whirling a body attached to a string, a ___ force is originated.
An attractive force exists between the sun and the earth in a direction ___ to the path of the earth.
The ___ attractive force between the nucleus and the electron acts as the required centripetal force.
When a car turns in a circular path or a curve, a ___ force exists between the road and the car tires.
When a car moves in a circular path which is inclined to the horizontal, the reaction force of the road acts ___ to the car.
The ___ component of normal force is the required centripetal force.
The upward thrust (lifting force) acts normally to an ___ body.
When the aeroplane tilts, the horizontal component acts normally to the motion of the aeroplane, causing the aeroplane to turn in a ___.
On a flat road (unbanked road), this force is supplied by ___ between the tires and the road.
If the frictional force is not sufficient, such as under wet and slippery conditions, the car will ___ out of the circular path.
Banking of roads is raising the ___ edge of the curved road higher than the inner edge.
The angle that the inclined track makes with horizontal is called angle of ___.
The normal force $F_N$ exerted by a banked road is ___ to the road.
Since there is no vertical motion, the net force on car in ___ direction is zero.
According to Eq.(2.5), the banking angle of a road $\theta$ is chosen so that this condition holds for the ___ speed $v$.
Centripetal force is the force acting towards the centre of the circular path around which the object is moving whereas the ___ force appears to act in a direction pointing away from the centre.
According to Newton's third law the ball exerts a ___ force on the hand.
Actually, the centrifugal force is not a real force. It is an apparent or ___ force which is equal and opposite to the centripetal force.
A centrifuge is a piece of equipment that puts an object in rotation around a fixed ___.
